news_list.title: Korea's Earth observation satellite makes first contact with home ground station
news_list.summary:
Korea's mid-sized Earth observation satellite has made its first contact with a ground station on home soil, the Korea AeroSpace Administration (KASA) said on Sunday.

The Next Generation Medium Satellite 2, also known as CAS500-2, established contact with the Korea Aerospace Research Institute ground station in Daejeon at 10:18 p.m. on Sunday, approximately 6 hours and 18 minutes after launch.

The satellite, developed by Korea Aerospace Industries, is expected to begin full operations in the second half of 2026, providing high-precision ground observation imagery for land and resource management, disaster response and national spatial information.

It was launched at 11:59 p.m. Saturday from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket. It separated from the launch vehicle approximately one hour after launch at an altitude of about 498 kilometers (309 miles), and made first contact with the ground station in Svalbard, Norway, around 15 minutes later, confirming that the main body systems were in good condition.

Engineers in Daejeon conducted a status check of the satellite, KASA said. Continuous monitoring of the satellite's condition will be carried out through further communications with both overseas and domestic ground stations.

To maintain round-the-clock contact with the satellite during the initial operating period, three overseas ground stations are reportedly being used in conjunction, including the Svalbard Satellite Station in Norway and stations at the Troll base in Antarctica.

The satellite, with a mission lifespan of four years, can observe the ground at a resolution of 0.5 meters (1.6 feet) in black and white and 2 meters in color. KASA said the imagery will be used for land and resource management, mapmaking, urban planning and ground change detection, and is also expected to support the observation of and response to natural disasters, including typhoons, heavy snowfall, floods and wildfires.

BY LEE JIAN [[email protected]]

news_list.title: Phayao Lake Facing An Ecological Disaster Due To Massive Drought - Chiang Rai Times
news_list.summary:
PHAYAO - Local authorities and residents in Phayao, Thailand, are facing a severe environmental crisis at Kwan Phayao, the province's most vital lake. An extreme drought has drastically reduced water levels, triggering a massive fish die-off and creating a bizarre "two-color" water phenomenon.

This ongoing emergency has sparked widespread concern because the rapidly decaying water quality threatens the region's drinking water supply, local fishing livelihoods, and the broader ecosystem. While the split waters might look striking at first glance, the underlying reality is a lethal lack of oxygen that is slowly suffocating one of Northern Thailand's most important freshwater resources.

Currently, the water level at Kwan Phayao has dropped to a highly alarming 390.35 meters. This leaves the lake with just under 15 million cubic meters of water. This volume is far below the safe capacity needed to keep the lake healthy.

Because of this massive drop, a rare and troubling visual event has occurred. The water has split into two distinct shades. One side of the lake is a murky, dark black, while the other side is a cloudy white.

While this sharp color contrast might look interesting to an outside observer, experts warn that it is a clear sign of deep trouble. The dark and cloudy waters mean the lake is polluted and rotting. Furthermore, this change shows a severe drop in the water's oxygen levels. Without enough oxygen, underwater life simply cannot survive.

The most heartbreaking result of this low oxygen is the massive number of dead fish. Across the vast lake, countless fish of various species have floated to the surface. As these fish die and begin to decay under the hot sun, a very strong, foul smell has spread across the entire area.

Local workers and officials are working around the clock to clean up the mess. They are using boats to scoop up the dead fish as fast as they can. However, the problem is growing faster than they can manage. New dead fish appear every single day. The terrible smell is not just a nuisance; it is causing real daily distress for the people who live near the water.

Beyond the bad smell and dying wildlife, there is a much bigger human problem. Kwan Phayao is not just a pretty landmark; it is the main source of raw water used to make tap water for the whole province.

When the raw water from the lake gets this dirty and polluted, it directly impacts the tap water system. Here is what residents might face if the problem continues:

Because of these risks, water treatment plants are under extreme pressure to filter and clean the water before it reaches homes.

The impact of this drought and water crisis goes far beyond the edge of the lake. The sudden loss of so many fish is a direct hit to the local fishermen. These workers rely on the lake for their daily income and food. With the fish dying out, their livelihoods are in danger.

Moreover, the entire local food chain is breaking down. When the small fish die, the larger animals and birds that eat them also suffer. If the dirty water continues to sit under the hot sun, it might even cause toxic algae to grow. This type of algae would make the water even more poisonous, killing whatever life is left.

Tourism, which brings a lot of money into the province, is also taking a hit. Visitors usually come to Kwan Phayao to enjoy the beautiful views and eat fresh seafood. Now, the bad smell and the sight of dead fish are keeping tourists away. Local farmers who use the lake's water for their crops are also feeling the pinch of the ongoing dry spell.

According to recent reports from local Thai media, government agencies are trying their best to manage the crisis. They are constantly checking the water quality and speeding up the removal of dead fish. You can read more about the original local report on this crisis directly from MGR Online.

However, cleaning up the fish is only a temporary fix. The real cure is fresh water, which relies entirely on rainfall. Until heavy rains return to refill the lake, the situation remains a waiting game. People in Phayao are very worried. They fear that if the dry weather lasts much longer, the province will face a massive water shortage.
news_list.source: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times

news_list.title: Investors are running out of time to brace for true oil shock
news_list.summary:
IN thrall to an artificial intelligence (AI) boom that has sent stocks to record highs and harbouring hopes of a short-lived Iran war, investors have yet to prepare for a doubling of physical oil prices.

The window to do so may soon be closing. There are plenty of reasons for market confidence, largely centred on the AI galaxy of hyperscalers, semiconductor makers and software developers and robust earnings growth.

The S&P 500 hit fresh record highs last Thursday.

While price pressures are showing up in business activity surveys and consumer inflation expectations, growth and employment remain on a fairly even keel and global central banks suggest they will not rush to raise interest rates as they weigh the impact of the war.

The part of the energy landscape where the real issue lurks is the physical market, where actual barrels of crude and refined products change hands, rather than electronic futures.

At around US$130 per barrel, prices here are some 70% higher than where they were in February, whether that is North Sea Forties, Angolan Cabinda or Norwegian Troll.

This reflects much higher energy costs for the world economy than implied by Brent crude futures, which are trading around US$110 a barrel, 50% higher than at the end of February.

Brent for delivery in 12 months' time is above US$80 a barrel, 20% above late-February levels.

"The physical markets reflect the reality on the ground and the futures market reflects more perceptions and hopes," said Tamas Varga, an analyst at energy broker PVM Oil Associates.

"One might say that physical markets are the true reflection of actually what's happening around the Strait of Hormuz."

A billion barrels gone

The war has effectively shuttered the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of global energy supplies flow.

Vitol, the world's largest oil trader, estimates one billion barrels in supply could be lost by the time the market recovers.

Fatih Birol, the head of the International Energy Agency, said in April that oil prices did not reflect the current situation and the world should prepare for much higher prices.

According to RBC Wealth Management head of investment strategy Frederique Carrier, a rule of thumb used by the firm's chief economist is that an oil shock needs to last between three and six months to have a sustainable impact on inflation.

"And we're not quite there in that window - we (will be) soon," she said, adding that her firm was neutral on equities, but favoured commodity-linked plays, such as shipping and warehousing.

Oil traders are stress-testing their books against a scenario in which crude prices hit US$200 to US$300, global commodity trading house Gunvor Group executive Jeff Webster told the FT Global Commodities Summit earlier in April.

"The idea that it's definitely going to be stagflation, or it's going to be fine. That's the bit that we're finding a little bit surprising," said Andrew Chorlton, chief investment officer for public fixed income at M&G, referring to a toxic mix of high inflation and weak economic growth.

"That seems a little complacent."

He said he had become "more tactical" on fixed income, looking at the divergence between countries or government bond yield curves.

Consumer inflation expectations are picking up. So are market-based indicators, such as inflation swaps, which show investors see US inflation around 3.53% in one year and around 2.75% in five years, above the Federal Reserve's 2% target.

These measures were closer to 2.4% in February, before the war erupted, LSEG data shows. It's a similar picture in the eurozone and UK.

Nuveen global investment strategist Laura Cooper said her firm still had AI tech exposure given its profitability, but was countering that with "dividend growers", infrastructure and real assets such as real estate and gold miners as a hedge against risks.

Long-term trends at risk?

No matter how big the disruption, markets eventually reprice associated risks, supply chains adapt, volatility subsides and investors return their focus to the big, long-term trends.

"You won't know it's a tipping point till the market reacts to it," said Paras Gupta, who manages discretionary portfolios for ultra-high-net-worth individuals in Asia for UBP in Singapore. "We just have to wait and see and be nimble. Everybody has one finger on the trigger."

The major risk with the Iran crisis lies in shifts in those precise long-term themes, analysts said.

In under 18 months, the Trump administration has shaken up global trade and international relations, generating near-unprecedented levels of uncertainty about America's reliability as an economic and security partner.

"This is about much more than when the war will be over, but rather about how the "Rupture" is playing out - shifting policy as well as public attitudes," said Tina Fordham, founder of political strategy consultancy Fordham Global.

"By the time geopolitical risks make landfall and hit financial markets, it is typically too late to mitigate them." -- Reuters

Ankur Banerjee and Amanda Cooper write for Reuters. The views expressed here are the writers' own.

news_list.title: IGB expands property, hospitality pipeline
news_list.summary:
PETALING JAYA: IGB Bhd is expecting its property development and hospitality businesses to contribute a larger portion to its revenue mix in 2026, underpinned by its continued sales in Southpoint Residences and the Visit Malaysia 2026 initiative.

In its newly released 2025 annual report, the group's chief executive officer Tan Boon Lee (pic) said IGB remains positive on its prospects as it is positioned in sectors backed by growth drivers, including government initiatives to promote foreign direct investment and tourism.

"We will continue to undertake asset enhancement initiatives to ensure our properties remain contemporary and aligned with evolving preferences, while improving operational efficiency and reducing our environmental footprint."

The group also plans to actively pursue opportunities for growth both domestically and across the region, expanding its footprint and diversification towards a more balanced portfolio mix.

A new hotel is also under construction as part of its mixed-use development in Southkey, Johor.

For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25), IGB saw its revenue rise to RM1.91bil from RM1.67bil in FY24. It recorded a net profit of RM361.02mil for FY25, down from RM416.22mil in the year before.

In 2025, the group took steps to strengthen its development pipeline by growing its landbank reserves with strategic acquisitions.

This included its commitment to acquire two parcels of leasehold land totalling 19.7 acres adjacent to its Mid Valley Southkey development in Johor and entering into an agreement to buy two freehold land parcels in Ipoh, Perak, totalling 24.3 acres.

"In parallel, we are exploring opportunities in emerging sectors such as aged care, industrial development, co-living, student accommodation, and education, while leveraging our established strengths in integrated mixed-use developments," Tan said.

Malaysia's economy's growth momentum is expected to remain steady in spite of intensified global geopolitical conflict and economic volatility, the group predicted.

A healthy labour market and government initiatives under Budget 2026, such as aid from Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah, are also projected to drive domestic consumption.

Meanwhile, investment activity is anticipated to remain strong, supported by high-growth initiatives like the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone and the National Semiconductor Strategy.

Additionally, continued expansion of the digital economy and tourism recovery is primed to further bolster the nation's economic prospects.

Tan commented that amid evolving consumer preferences, demand continues to rise for integrated community hubs that feature a diverse mix of services, accommodation, and commercial offerings.

"With our depth of experience, we believe the group is well-positioned to capitalise on these opportunities and be recognised as a leading Malaysian developer in the mixed-use development sector," he said.

"IGB remains aligned with shareholder interests and is committed to delivering sustainable long-term value through disciplined growth, strategic portfolio management, and the continued monetisation of mature assets to unlock value for shareholders."

themes.name: 聯準會利率政策與強勢美元主導市場情緒
themes.insight: 市場焦點集中在美國聯準會維持高利率的決策，此舉支撐美元持續走強，並對新興市場債券、海外基金等全球資產配置策略產生深遠影響。
evidence: FED維持利率不變，支撐美元...

themes.name: 實體原油市場拉警報，地緣政治引發潛在石油危機
themes.insight: 由於地緣政治緊張，實體原油價格已遠超期貨市場，暗示潛在的「石油衝擊」風險。分析警告油價可能大幅飆升，但市場在人工智慧熱潮中可能過於自滿而忽略此風險。
evidence: Investors are running out of time to brace for true oil shock

themes.name: 東南亞經濟兩樣情：大馬樂觀發展對比泰國乾旱危機
themes.insight: 東南亞市場呈現分歧格局。馬來西亞在政府政策（如2026大馬旅遊年）推動下，房地產與觀光業前景看好；與此同時，泰國正因嚴重乾旱引發重大生態與經濟危機，威脅供水、漁業及旅遊業。
sources: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times

In 2025, the group took steps to strengthen its development pipeline by growing its landbank reserves with strategic acquisitions.

This included its commitment to acquire two parcels of leasehold land totalling 19.7 acres adjacent to its Mid Valley Southkey development in Johor and entering into an agreement to buy two freehold land parcels in Ipoh, Perak, totalling 24.3 acres.

"In parallel, we are exploring opportunities in emerging sectors such as aged care, industrial development, co-living, student accommodation, and education, while leveraging our established strengths in integrated mixed-use developments," Tan said.

Malaysia's economy's growth momentum is expected to remain steady in spite of intensified global geopolitical conflict and economic volatility, the group predicted.

A healthy labour market and government initiatives under Budget 2026, such as aid from Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah, are also projected to drive domestic consumption.

Meanwhile, investment activity is anticipated to remain strong, supported by high-growth initiatives like the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone and the National Semiconductor Strategy.

Additionally, continued expansion of the digital economy and tourism recovery is primed to further bolster the nation's economic prospects.

Tan commented that amid evolving consumer preferences, demand continues to rise for integrated community hubs that feature a diverse mix of services, accommodation, and commercial offerings.

"With our depth of experience, we believe the group is well-positioned to capitalise on these opportunities and be recognised as a leading Malaysian developer in the mixed-use development sector," he said.

"IGB remains aligned with shareholder interests and is committed to delivering sustainable long-term value through disciplined growth, strategic portfolio management, and the continued monetisation of mature assets to unlock value for shareholders."

Phayao Lake Facing An Ecological Disaster Due To Massive Drought - Chiang Rai Times

ai_analysis.full_text:
---
【今日結論】
今日開盤預計承壓，盤中焦點將是權值股的角力。儘管美股與鄰近亞股走強，但台積電 ADR 高達 18% 的溢價是市場最大的提款機隱憂，加上台指期夜盤收黑，顯示開盤後獲利了結與套利賣壓將相當沉重。
【新聞驅動】
市場主線是「AI 狂熱」與「通膨警報」的對決。一方面，DRAM 短缺等 AI 需求持續為相關供應鏈帶來利多；但另一方面，布蘭特原油已站上 107 美元的「需求破壞」警戒線，這不只是短期波動，而是可能衝擊全球經濟與企業獲利的結構性風險，市場對通膨的擔憂正在加劇。
【國際與台股】
國際氛圍偏多，上週五美股科技股領漲，今早開盤的日經與 KOSPI 也同步走強，顯示亞洲資金動能尚在。然而，台股面臨自身壓力，台積電 ADR 溢價過高是首要風險，暗示其現貨開盤後有強烈修正壓力；此外，從先前盤面數據觀察，下跌家數遠多於上漲家數，顯示盤面結構並不健康，需提防指數被權值股拉抬的虛胖現象。
【KOL與情緒】
市場情緒呈現「表面貪婪，內心焦慮」的矛盾狀態。雖然台美股市的恐懼貪婪指數都處於貪婪區間，但社群熱搜關鍵字中「儲蓄」的熱度提升，透露出散戶可能已在尋找避險管道或轉趨保守，這股潛在的防禦心態，將使追價意願降低。
【操作含意】
今日操作的決策帶寬狹窄，關鍵在於觀察台積電開盤後，市場如何消化巨大的溢價。與其預測指數漲跌，不如關注開盤後的賣壓是否擴散至其他 AI 相關族群。在原油價格這個中線烏雲籠罩下，資金可能會從高位階電子股流向防禦或傳產族群，建議保持謹慎，待開盤壓力測試過後再做決策。

selected_charts.title: 美股與風險資產
selected_charts.description: S&P 500、Nasdaq、SOX、VIX 與美元、黃金、原油的跨資產訊號。
selected_charts.takeaway: 美股由科技股領漲，但布蘭特原油已站上 107 美元警戒線，VIX 恐慌指數也微幅上升至 16.99，顯示市場在 AI 的樂觀情緒中，仍對潛在的通膨與地緣政治風險保持警戒。
selected_charts.action: 觀察油價是否持續攀升，以及其對科技股估值的壓力測試。

selected_charts.title: 盤前指標
selected_charts.description: Nasdaq 期貨、台積電 ADR、台灣 ETF 與台股盤前連動。
selected_charts.takeaway: 儘管台積電 ADR 上漲 0.41%，但其相對於台股的溢價已高達 18%，加上台指期夜盤收黑，預示今日開盤將面臨沉重的套利與獲利了結賣壓。
selected_charts.action: 關注開盤後權值股的走勢，特別是台積電是否出現因 ADR 套利引發的賣壓。

selected_charts.title: 美股分析師共識圖
selected_charts.description: Seeking Alpha 的 Quant、作者與華爾街評級共識，搭配多頭/空頭強弱排序。
selected_charts.takeaway: Seeking Alpha 分析師對大型科技股的看法分歧，其中對亞馬遜(AMZN)、Google(GOOGL)和 Meta(META) 的共識評級最高，而特斯拉(TSLA)共識相對較低，顯示市場資金可能更集中於特定的 AI 受益股。
selected_charts.action: 留意分析師共識較高的強勢股是否能延續動能，帶動相關供應鏈。
